description: A bill spike from routing all private egress and AWS-service traffic through a NAT Gateway.
body: Failure mode: every private subnet routes through one NAT Gateway, so high-volume traffic to S3/DynamoDB/ECR incurs NAT data-processing and cross-AZ charges, spiking the bill. Detection signals: Cost Explorer NatGateway-Bytes and DataProcessing dominating, traffic to AWS services flowing over NAT, single-AZ NAT serving cross-AZ subnets. Do not just accept the cost or delete the NAT that other traffic needs. Safer fallback: add VPC Gateway endpoints for S3/DynamoDB and Interface endpoints for ECR/other services, deploy a NAT per AZ to avoid cross-AZ data charges, and set a budget alarm.
